Ranking and metric objectives are directly aligned with recommender ranking; common strength is defensible synthesis.
Linear models are plausible baselines/components for recommendation and ranking; common strength is defensible.
k-NN is historically used in collaborative filtering; optional strength is defensible, while Naive Bayes is less central.
Clustering/mixtures can support segmentation or candidate organization; optional strength is defensible.
